author: nflaig
content: I think I permanently damaged dora for this block/attestations view https://dora.glamsterdam-devnet-5.ethpandaops.io/slot/37917#attestations

author: nflaig
content: does a restart maybe fix it?

author: nflaig
content: it happens if I click into the attestations view and then out of it into a different tab, but not exactly sure about the exact trigger that causes this

author: nflaig
content: also it seems like indices are no longer mapped to client labels

author: pk910
content: The underlying issue is that there is a "null" passed as validator names lookup dict..  So there is a JS exception thrown that kills the client side rendering

author: pk910
content: nice find o.O  it's a bug in my ssz lib 😅

author: nflaig
content: thanks, it works now, but do you know why we don't see the labels anymorehttps://dora.glamsterdam-devnet-5.ethpandaops.io/slot/37917#attestations

author: pk910
content:
yea,  the attester duties are not persisted in the dora db.  that'd be megabytes of data for every epoch for mainnet sized networks.
So the duties are kept in memory only for the most recent 2 or 3 epochs.  Afterwards it's dropped off.
Anything building on top of the duties (like the attestestation bitmask lookups) is then no longer available

author: pk910
content:
I could probably store them in S3 or so to not clutter the DB 🤔 
Would it be helpful to have it available long term?

content: the reason I am doing it right now is because we stopped accepting deathstar blocks as canonical (which is good) https://dora.glamsterdam-devnet-5.ethpandaops.io/slots/filtered?f&f.missing=1&f.orphaned=1&f.pname=lodestar-ethrex-1 but I wanna understand why, the theory right now is because grandine is offline and before in some cases they added enough attestation weight (since they voted incorrectly together with Nimbus) with proposer boost to flip the head, so the proposer that build on top of the deathstar branch in some cases are actually correct, but this can only happen if we have ~20% malicious attesters supporting it cc <@755590043632140352> does that explain it?
